=== app.py ===
from flask import Flask, request, session, g, redirect, url_for, render_template, abort
import os
from datetime import datetime

# Data directory and filenames
DATA_DIR = 'data'
USERS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')
ARTICLES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'articles.txt')
ARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'article_versions.txt')
APPROVALS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'approvals.txt')
COMMENTS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'comments.txt')
ANALYTICS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'analytics.txt')
WORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'workflow_stages.txt')

# Utility functions for file I/O and data parsing

def read_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames):
    records = []
    if not os.path.exists(filename):
        return records
    with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for line in f:
            line=line.strip('\n\r ')
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split('|')
            if len(parts) != len(fieldnames):
                # skip malformed line
                continue
            record = dict(zip(fieldnames, parts))
            records.append(record)
    return records


def append_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, record_dict):
    values = []
    for field in fieldnames:
        val = record_dict.get(field, '')
        if val is None:
            val = ''
        if isinstance(val, str):
            val = val.replace('\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')
        values.append(str(val))
    with open(filename, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('|'.join(values)+'\n')


def overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, records):
    with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for rec in records:
            values = []
            for field in fieldnames:
                val = rec.get(field, '')
                if val is None:
                    val = ''
                if isinstance(val, str):
                    val = val.replace('\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')
                values.append(str(val))
            f.write('|'.join(values)+'\n')


# Models

# User model
class User:
    FIELDS = ['username', 'fullname', 'email']

    def __init__(self, username, fullname, email):
        self.username = username
        self.fullname = fullname
        self.email = email

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        users = read_pipe_delimited_file(USERS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        return [cls(**u) for u in users]

    @classmethod
    def find_by_username(cls, username):
        all_users = cls.load_all()
        for user in all_users:
            if user.username == username:
                return user
        return None


# Article model
class Article:
    # Fields as per articles.txt
    # article_id|title|category|author|status|created_at|updated_at
    FIELDS = ['article_id','title','category','author','status','created_at','updated_at']

    def __init__(self, article_id, title, category, author, status, created_at, updated_at):
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.title = title
        self.category = category
        self.author = author
        self.status = status
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.updated_at = updated_at

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        articles = read_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLES_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for a in articles:
            try:
                result.append(Article(**a))
            except Exception:
                # malformed line
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def find_by_id(cls, article_id):
        if not isinstance(article_id, int):
            try:
                article_id = int(article_id)
            except:
                return None
        for a in cls.load_all():
            if a.article_id == article_id:
                return a
        return None

    @classmethod
    def find_by_author(cls, author):
        return [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.author == author]

    @classmethod
    def find_published(cls, category_filter=None, sort_key=None):
        articles = [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.status == 'published']
        if category_filter:
            articles = [a for a in articles if a.category == category_filter]
        if sort_key == 'created_at':
            articles.sort(key=lambda x:x.created_at, reverse=True)
        elif sort_key == 'title':
            articles.sort(key=lambda x:x.title)
        return articles

    @classmethod
    def next_article_id(cls):
        articles = cls.load_all()
        if not articles:
            return 1
        max_id = max(a.article_id for a in articles)
        return max_id + 1

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'article_id': self.article_id,
            'title': self.title,
            'category': self.category,
            'author': self.author,
            'status': self.status,
            'created_at': self.created_at,
            'updated_at': self.updated_at
        }

    def save(self):
        all_articles = Article.load_all()
        updated = False
        for i,a in enumerate(all_articles):
            if a.article_id == self.article_id:
                all_articles[i] = self
                updated = True
                break
        if not updated:
            all_articles.append(self)
        # Write back
        records = [a.to_dict() for a in all_articles]
        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLES_FILE, Article.FIELDS, records)


# ArticleVersion model
class ArticleVersion:
    # article_versions.txt fields:
    # version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|change_summary
    FIELDS = ['version_id','article_id','version_number','content','author','timestamp','change_summary']

    def __init__(self, version_id, article_id, version_number, content, author, timestamp, change_summary):
        self.version_id = int(version_id)
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.version_number = int(version_number)
        self.content = content
        self.author = author
        self.timestamp = timestamp
        self.change_summary = change_summary

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(ArticleVersion(**rec))
            except Exception:
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def find_by_article(cls, article_id):
        return [v for v in cls.load_all() if v.article_id == int(article_id)]

    @classmethod
    def find_latest_version_number(cls, article_id):
        versions = cls.find_by_article(article_id)
        if not versions:
            return 0
        return max(v.version_number for v in versions)

    @classmethod
    def find_by_version_id(cls, version_id):
        for v in cls.load_all():
            if v.version_id == int(version_id):
                return v
        return None

    @classmethod
    def next_version_id(cls):
        versions = cls.load_all()
        if not versions:
            return 1
        return max(v.version_id for v in versions) + 1

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'version_id': self.version_id,
            'article_id': self.article_id,
            'version_number': self.version_number,
            'content': self.content,
            'author': self.author,
            'timestamp': self.timestamp,
            'change_summary': self.change_summary
        }

    def save(self):
        versions = ArticleVersion.load_all()
        updated = False
        for i,v in enumerate(versions):
            if v.version_id == self.version_id:
                versions[i] = self
                updated = True
                break
        if not updated:
            versions.append(self)
        records = [v.to_dict() for v in versions]
        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE, ArticleVersion.FIELDS, records)


# Approval model
class Approval:
    # approvals.txt fields:
    # approval_id|article_id|version_id|approver|status|comments|timestamp
    FIELDS = ['approval_id','article_id','version_id','approver','status','comments','timestamp']

    def __init__(self, approval_id, article_id, version_id, approver, status, comments, timestamp):
        self.approval_id = int(approval_id)
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.version_id = int(version_id)
        self.approver = approver
        self.status = status
        self.comments = comments
        self.timestamp = timestamp

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(APPROVALS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(Approval(**rec))
            except Exception:
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def find_by_article_and_version(cls, article_id, version_id):
        return [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.article_id == int(article_id) and a.version_id == int(version_id)]

    @classmethod
    def next_approval_id(cls):
        approvals = cls.load_all()
        if not approvals:
            return 1
        return max(a.approval_id for a in approvals) + 1

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'approval_id': self.approval_id,
            'article_id': self.article_id,
            'version_id': self.version_id,
            'approver': self.approver,
            'status': self.status,
            'comments': self.comments,
            'timestamp': self.timestamp
        }

    def save(self):
        approvals = Approval.load_all()
        updated = False
        for i,a in enumerate(approvals):
            if a.approval_id == self.approval_id:
                approvals[i] = self
                updated = True
                break
        if not updated:
            approvals.append(self)
        records = [a.to_dict() for a in approvals]
        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(APPROVALS_FILE, Approval.FIELDS, records)


# Comment model
class Comment:
    # comments.txt fields:
    # comment_id|article_id|version_id|commenter|comment_text|timestamp
    FIELDS = ['comment_id','article_id','version_id','commenter','comment_text','timestamp']

    def __init__(self, comment_id, article_id, version_id, commenter, comment_text, timestamp):
        self.comment_id = int(comment_id)
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.version_id = int(version_id)
        self.commenter = commenter
        self.comment_text = comment_text
        self.timestamp = timestamp

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(COMMENTS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(Comment(**rec))
            except Exception:
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def find_by_article_and_version(cls, article_id, version_id):
        return [c for c in cls.load_all() if c.article_id == int(article_id) and c.version_id == int(version_id)]

    @classmethod
    def next_comment_id(cls):
        comments = cls.load_all()
        if not comments:
            return 1
        return max(c.comment_id for c in comments) + 1

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'comment_id': self.comment_id,
            'article_id': self.article_id,
            'version_id': self.version_id,
            'commenter': self.commenter,
            'comment_text': self.comment_text,
            'timestamp': self.timestamp
        }

    def save(self):
        comments = Comment.load_all()
        updated = False
        for i,c in enumerate(comments):
            if c.comment_id == self.comment_id:
                comments[i] = self
                updated = True
                break
        if not updated:
            comments.append(self)
        records = [c.to_dict() for c in comments]
        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(COMMENTS_FILE, Comment.FIELDS, records)


# Analytics model
class AnalyticsEntry:
    # analytics.txt fields:
    # entry_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares
    FIELDS = ['entry_id','article_id','date','views','unique_visitors','avg_time_seconds','shares']

    def __init__(self, entry_id, article_id, date, views, unique_visitors, avg_time_seconds, shares):
        self.entry_id = int(entry_id)
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.date = date
        self.views = int(views)
        self.unique_visitors = int(unique_visitors)
        self.avg_time_seconds = float(avg_time_seconds)
        self.shares = int(shares)

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(ANALYTICS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(AnalyticsEntry(**rec))
            except Exception:
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def find_by_article(cls, article_id):
        return [e for e in cls.load_all() if e.article_id == int(article_id)]


# WorkflowStage model (not explicitly used in routes, but available)
class WorkflowStage:
    # workflow_stages.txt fields:
    # stage_id|category|stage_order|description
    FIELDS = ['stage_id','category','stage_order','description']

    def __init__(self, stage_id, category, stage_order, description):
        self.stage_id = int(stage_id)
        self.category = category
        self.stage_order = int(stage_order)
        self.description = description

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(WORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE, cls.FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(WorkflowStage(**rec))
            except:
                continue
        return result


# Business logic services


class ArticleService:
    @staticmethod
    def create_article(title, category, author):
        new_id = Article.next_article_id()
        now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        article = Article(
            article_id=new_id,
            title=title,
            category=category,
            author=author,
            status='draft',
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now
        )
        article.save()
        # Create initial version empty with version_number 1
        version = ArticleVersion(
            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),
            article_id=new_id,
            version_number=1,
            content='',
            author=author,
            timestamp=now,
            change_summary="Initial version"
        )
        version.save()
        return article

    @staticmethod
    def update_article(article_id, title, category):
        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)
        if not article:
            return None
        article.title = title
        article.category = category
        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        article.save()
        return article


class VersionService:
    @staticmethod
    def create_version(article_id, content, author, change_summary):
        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)
        if not article:
            return None
        next_version_num = ArticleVersion.find_latest_version_number(article_id) + 1
        version = ArticleVersion(
            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),
            article_id=article_id,
            version_number=next_version_num,
            content=content,
            author=author,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            change_summary=change_summary
        )
        version.save()
        # Update article updated_at
        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        article.save()
        return version

    @staticmethod
    def get_versions(article_id):
        return sorted(ArticleVersion.find_by_article(article_id), key=lambda v: v.version_number)


class ApprovalService:
    @staticmethod
    def add_approval(article_id, version_id, approver, status, comments):
        approval_id = Approval.next_approval_id()
        approval = Approval(
            approval_id=approval_id,
            article_id=article_id,
            version_id=version_id,
            approver=approver,
            status=status,
            comments=comments,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        )
        approval.save()
        return approval

    @staticmethod
    def get_approvals(article_id, version_id):
        return Approval.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)


class CommentService:
    @staticmethod
    def add_comment(article_id, version_id, commenter, comment_text):
        comment_id = Comment.next_comment_id()
        comment = Comment(
            comment_id=comment_id,
            article_id=article_id,
            version_id=version_id,
            commenter=commenter,
            comment_text=comment_text,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        )
        comment.save()
        return comment

    @staticmethod
    def get_comments(article_id, version_id):
        return Comment.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)


class AnalyticsService:
    @staticmethod
    def get_analytics_for_article(article_id):
        analytics_entries = AnalyticsEntry.find_by_article(article_id)
        # Aggregate overall data
        total_views = sum(entry.views for entry in analytics_entries)
        total_unique = sum(entry.unique_visitors for entry in analytics_entries)
        average_time = 0
        if analytics_entries:
            average_time = sum(entry.avg_time_seconds * entry.views for entry in analytics_entries) / max(total_views,1)
        total_shares = sum(entry.shares for entry in analytics_entries)
        return {
            'total_views': total_views,
            'total_unique_visitors': total_unique,
            'average_time_seconds': round(average_time, 2),
            'total_shares': total_shares,
            'daily_data': sorted(analytics_entries, key=lambda e: e.date)
        }


# Simple input validation (only very basic validations)

def validate_article_form(form):
    errors = {}
    title = form.get('title','').strip()
    category = form.get('category','').strip()
    if not title:
        errors['title'] = "Title cannot be empty."
    if not category:
        errors['category'] = "Category cannot be empty."
    # TODO: add more validation if needed
    return errors


def validate_schedule_form(form):
    errors = {}
    # example: scheduled_date, article_id
    scheduled_date = form.get('scheduled_date','').strip()
    article_id = form.get('article_id','').strip()
    if not scheduled_date:
        errors['scheduled_date'] = "Scheduled date is required."
    if not article_id:
        errors['article_id'] = "Article ID is required."
    else:
        try:
            int(article_id)
        except:
            errors['article_id'] = "Article ID must be a number."
    return errors


# Flask app factory
app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'supersecretkey'


# Before request - set user in g from session
@app.before_request
def load_logged_in_user():
    g.user = None
    username = session.get('username')
    if username:
        user = User.find_by_username(username)
        if user:
            g.user = user


# Root redirect
@app.route('/')
def root_redirect():
    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))


# Route: /dashboard
@app.route('/dashboard')
def dashboard():
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    return render_template('dashboard.html', username=g.user.username)


# Route: /article/create (GET, POST)
@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET','POST'])
def create_article():
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = request.form
        errors = validate_article_form(form)
        if errors:
            return render_template('article_create.html', validation_errors=errors, form=form)
        title = form.get('title','').strip()
        category = form.get('category','').strip()
        author = g.user.username
        article = ArticleService.create_article(title, category, author)
        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article.article_id))
    else:
        return render_template('article_create.html')


# Route: /article/<int:article_id>/edit (GET, POST)
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/edit', methods=['GET','POST'])
def edit_article(article_id):
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)
    if not article:
        abort(404)
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = request.form
        errors = validate_article_form(form)
        if errors:
            # Provide article data as well
            return render_template('article_edit.html', validation_errors=errors, article=article, form=form)

        # update article main info
        updated_article = ArticleService.update_article(article_id, form.get('title','').strip(), form.get('category','').strip())

        # Create new version if content or change_summary provided
        content = form.get('content','').strip()
        change_summary = form.get('change_summary','').strip()

        if content or change_summary:
            if not change_summary:
                change_summary = "Updated content"
            VersionService.create_version(article_id, content, g.user.username, change_summary)

        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))

    else:
        # Show latest version content if any
        versions = VersionService.get_versions(article_id)
        latest_version = versions[-1] if versions else None
        return render_template('article_edit.html', article=article, current_version=latest_version)


# Route: /article/<int:article_id>/versions (GET)
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/versions')
def article_version_history(article_id):
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)
    if not article:
        abort(404)
    versions = VersionService.get_versions(article_id)
    # we can allow optional comparison query param but not in scope
    return render_template('article_versions.html', article=article, versions=versions)


# Route: /articles/mine (GET)
@app.route('/articles/mine')
def my_articles():
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    # optional filters can be added - ignoring for now
    articles = Article.find_by_author(g.user.username)
    return render_template('articles_mine.html', articles=articles)


# Route: /articles/published (GET)
@app.route('/articles/published')
def published_articles():
    category = request.args.get('category', None)
    sort = request.args.get('sort', None)
    articles = Article.find_published(category_filter=category, sort_key=sort)
    return render_template('articles_published.html', articles=articles)


# Route: /calendar (GET, POST)
# Calendar scheduling is implied to be a feature that can store scheduled content
# Not specified in detail in architecture, assuming a schedule is stored in a file (e.g., scheduled.txt or calendar.txt). Since no file specified, we implement a basic calendar storage

CALENDAR_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'calendar.txt')
# calendar.txt fields:
# calendar_id|article_id|scheduled_date|notes
CALENDAR_FIELDS = ['calendar_id','article_id','scheduled_date','notes']


class CalendarEntry:
    def __init__(self, calendar_id, article_id, scheduled_date, notes):
        self.calendar_id = int(calendar_id)
        self.article_id = int(article_id)
        self.scheduled_date = scheduled_date
        self.notes = notes

    @classmethod
    def load_all(cls):
        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS)
        result = []
        for rec in records:
            try:
                result.append(CalendarEntry(**rec))
            except Exception:
                continue
        return result

    @classmethod
    def next_calendar_id(cls):
        entries = cls.load_all()
        if not entries:
            return 1
        return max(e.calendar_id for e in entries) + 1

    def to_dict(self):
        return {
            'calendar_id': self.calendar_id,
            'article_id': self.article_id,
            'scheduled_date': self.scheduled_date,
            'notes': self.notes
        }

    def save(self):
        entries = CalendarEntry.load_all()
        updated = False
        for i,e in enumerate(entries):
            if e.calendar_id == self.calendar_id:
                entries[i] = self
                updated = True
                break
        if not updated:
            entries.append(self)
        records = [e.to_dict() for e in entries]
        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS, records)


@app.route('/calendar', methods=['GET','POST'])
def content_calendar():
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    if request.method == 'POST':
        form = request.form
        errors = validate_schedule_form(form)
        if errors:
            scheduled_items = CalendarEntry.load_all()
            return render_template('calendar.html', validation_errors=errors, scheduled_items=scheduled_items)
        article_id = int(form.get('article_id').strip())
        scheduled_date = form.get('scheduled_date').strip()
        notes = form.get('notes','').strip()
        calendar_id = CalendarEntry.next_calendar_id()
        calendar_entry = CalendarEntry(calendar_id, article_id, scheduled_date, notes)
        calendar_entry.save()
        return redirect(url_for('content_calendar'))
    else:
        scheduled_items = CalendarEntry.load_all()
        return render_template('calendar.html', scheduled_items=scheduled_items)


# Route: /article/<int:article_id>/analytics (GET)
@app.route('/article/<int:article_id>/analytics')
def article_analytics(article_id):
    if not g.user:
        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))
    article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)
    if not article:
        abort(404)
    analytics_data = AnalyticsService.get_analytics_for_article(article_id)
    return render_template('article_analytics.html', article=article, analytics=analytics_data)


# Run the app if executed directly
if __name__ == '__main__':
    if not os.path.exists(DATA_DIR):
        os.makedirs(DATA_DIR)
    app.run(debug=True)

          <td>
            <form method="POST" action="{{ url_for('content_calendar') }}" style="display:inline;" id="delete-schedule-form-{{ item.id }}">
              <input type="hidden" name="delete_schedule_id" value="{{ item.id }}">
              <button type="submit" id="delete-schedule-{{ item.id }}">Delete</button>
            </form>
          </td>
        </tr>
        {% endfor %}
      </tbody>
    </table>
    {% else %}
    <p>No scheduled items.</p>
    {% endif %}

    <button type="button" id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="window.location.href='{{ url_for('dashboard') }}'">Back to Dashboard</button>
  </div>

=== feedback_testing.txt ===
Functional Testing Report for Content Publishing Hub

1. Dashboard Page
- Access without login redirects to root as expected.
- Access with login shows dashboard with div id="dashboard-page" and personalized welcome.

2. Article Creation (/article/create)
- GET loads create article page correctly.
- POST with empty title and category returns validation errors as expected with messages "Title cannot be empty.", "Category cannot be empty.".
- POST with valid data redirects to edit page with new article id included.

3. Article Editing (/article/<article_id>/edit)
- GET loads the edit article page with div id="edit-article-page".
- POST to update title, category, and new content creates a new version and updates main article.
- Redirect after POST is successful.
- Article title and latest version number updated correctly.

4. Version History (/article/<article_id>/versions)
- Loads version history page with div id="version-history-page" and table with id="version-history-table".
- Displays correct number of versions including newly added ones.

5. My Articles (/articles/mine)
- Shows table with id="my-articles-table" listing user's articles.
- Article entries with titles present.

6. Published Articles (/articles/published)
- Displays published articles filtered by category and sorted correctly.
- Table with id="published-articles-table" shown and data displayed.

7. Content Calendar (/calendar)
- GET loads content calendar page with div id="content-calendar-page".
- POST with missing scheduled_date shows validation error.
- POST with invalid article_id shows validation error.
- POST with valid data redirects and saves scheduled item.

8. Article Analytics (/article/<article_id>/analytics)
- Loads analytics page with div id="article-analytics-page".

Defects and Issues:
- Several backend operations that write files (e.g., creating articles or versions) failed due to missing data directory or files (FileNotFoundError for 'data/articles.txt' and similar).
- This implies that the data directory 'data' and necessary data files must exist before the app is used.
- The dashboard template refers to 'analytics_article_id' variable for analytics link but no such variable provided in route leading to possible rendering errors.
- Templates like content_calendar.html refer to variables like 'unscheduled_articles' and 'scheduled_items' with attributes like 'article_title' and 'schedule_date', but those are not fully supported or mapped in the current backend code.
- The article_analytics.html template uses variables 'analytics_data' in a loop but backend sends 'analytics' dictionary, mismatch in variable names may cause rendering issues.
- The analytics table in article_analytics.html shows columns for Comments and Approvals but there is no code populating 'comments' or 'approvals' counts in the analytics data passed, these columns may appear empty.

Suggestions:
- Ensure 'data' directory and all necessary files exist or are created on app startup or deployment.
- Fix the dashboard route to provide 'analytics_article_id' or adjust template to handle missing variable gracefully.
- Check content calendar data preparation to supply correct article lists for scheduling.
- Fix variable naming mismatch in article analytics template (use 'analytics.daily_data' for the loop if that holds data).
- Backend should enhance analytics data to include comments and approval counts or remove those columns from the analytics table.

Status: NEED_MODIFY

This report summarizes the main functional coverage and discovered defects to guide the ImplementationImprover for corrections.

records = []\n    if not os.path.exists(filename):\n        return records\n    with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line=line.strip('\\n\\r ')\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != len(fieldnames):\n                # skip malformed line\n                continue\n            record = dict(zip(fieldnames, parts))\n            records.append(record)\n    return records\n\n\ndef append_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, record_dict):\n    values = []\n    for field in fieldnames:\n        val = record_dict.get(field, '')\n        if val is None:\n            val = ''\n        if isinstance(val, str):\n            val = val.replace('\\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')\n        values.append(str(val))\n    with open(filename, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('|'.join(values)+'\\n')\n\n\ndef overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, records):\n    with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for rec in records:\n            values = []\n            for field in fieldnames:\n                val = rec.get(field, '')\n                if val is None:\n                    val = ''\n                if isinstance(val, str):\n                    val = val.replace('\\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')\n for user in all_users:\n            if user.username == username:\n                return user\n        return None\n\n\n# Article model\nclass Article:\n    # Fields as per articles.txt\n    # self.status = status\n        self.created_at = created_at\n        self.updated_at = updated_at\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        articles = read_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLES_FILE, int(article_id)\n            except:\n                return None\n        for a in cls.load_all():\n            if a.article_id == article_id:\n                return a\n        return None\n\n sort_key=None):\n        articles = [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.status == 'published']\n        if category_filter:\n            articles = [a for a in articles if a.category == category_filter]\n x:x.title)\n        return articles\n\n    @classmethod\n    def next_article_id(cls):\n        articles = cls.load_all()\n        if not articles:\n            return 1\n        max_id = max(a.article_id for a in articles)\n        return max_id + 1\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            'article_id': self.article_id,\n            'title': self.title,\n entry_id|article_id|date|views|unique_visitors|avg_time_seconds|shares\n    FIELDS = ['entry_id','article_id','date','views','unique_visitors','avg_time_seconds','shares']\n\n    def __init__(self, entry_id, article_id, date, views, unique_visitors, avg_time_seconds, shares):\n        self.entry_id = int(entry_id)\n        self.article_id = int(article_id)\n        self.date = date\n        self.views = int(views)\n        self.unique_visitors = int(unique_visitors)\n        self.avg_time_seconds = float(avg_time_seconds)\n        self.shares = int(shares)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(ANALYTICS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for rec in records:\n            try:\n                result.append(AnalyticsEntry(**rec))\n            except Exception:\n                continue\n        return result\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_article(cls, article_id):\n        return [e for e in cls.load_all() if e.article_id == int(article_id)]\n\n\n# WorkflowStage model (not explicitly used in routes, but available)\nclass WorkflowStage:\n    # workflow_stages.txt fields:\n    # stage_id|category|stage_order|description\n    FIELDS = ['stage_id','category','stage_order','description']\n\n    def __init__(self, stage_id, category, stage_order, description):\n        self.stage_id = int(stage_id)\n        self.category = category\n        self.stage_order = int(stage_order)\n        self.description = description\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(WORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for rec in records:\n            try:\n                result.append(WorkflowStage(**rec))\n            except:\n                continue\n        return result\n\n\n# Business logic services\n\n\nclass ArticleService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def create_article(title, category, author):\n        new_id = Article.next_article_id()\n        now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article = Article(\n            article_id=new_id,\n            title=title,\n            category=category,\n            author=author,\n            status='draft',\n            created_at=now,\n            updated_at=now\n        )\n        article.save()\n        # Create initial version empty with version_number 1\n        version = ArticleVersion(\n            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),\n            article_id=new_id,\n            version_number=1,\n            content='',\n            author=author,\n            timestamp=now,\n            change_summary=\"Initial version\"\n        )\n        version.save()\n        return article\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def update_article(article_id, title, category):\n        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)\n        if not article:\n            return None\n        article.title = title\n        article.category = category\n        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article.save()\n        return article\n\n\nclass VersionService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def create_version(article_id, content, author, change_summary):\n        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)\n        if not article:\n            return None\n        next_version_num = ArticleVersion.find_latest_version_number(article_id) + 1\n        version = ArticleVersion(\n            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_number=next_version_num,\n            content=content,\n            author=author,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            change_summary=change_summary\n        )\n        version.save()\n        # Update article updated_at\n        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article.save()\n        return version\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_versions(article_id):\n        return sorted(ArticleVersion.find_by_article(article_id), key=lambda v: v.version_number)\n\n\nclass ApprovalService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_approval(article_id, version_id, approver, status, comments):\n        approval_id = Approval.next_approval_id()\n        approval = Approval(\n            approval_id=approval_id,\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_id=version_id,\n            approver=approver,\n            status=status,\n            comments=comments,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        approval.save()\n        return approval\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_approvals(article_id, version_id):\n        return Approval.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)\n\n\nclass CommentService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_comment(article_id, version_id, commenter, comment_text):\n        comment_id = Comment.next_comment_id()\n        comment = Comment(\n            comment_id=comment_id,\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_id=version_id,\n            commenter=commenter,\n            comment_text=comment_text,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        comment.save()\n        return comment\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_comments(article_id, version_id):\n        return Comment.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)\n\n\nclass AnalyticsService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_analytics_for_article(article_id):\n        analytics_entries = AnalyticsEntry.find_by_article(article_id)\n        # Aggregate overall data\n        total_views = sum(entry.views for entry in analytics_entries)\n        total_unique = sum(entry.unique_visitors for entry in analytics_entries)\n        average_time = 0\n        if analytics_entries:\n            average_time = sum(entry.avg_time_seconds * entry.views for entry in analytics_entries) / max(total_views,1)\n        total_shares = sum(entry.shares for entry in analytics_entries)\n\n        # Calculate comments and approvals counts per date\n        comments = Comment.load_all()\n        approvals = Approval.load_all()\n\n        comment_counts = {}\n        approval_counts = {}\n\n        for c in comments:\n            if c.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = c.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                comment_counts[date_only] = comment_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        for a in approvals:\n            if a.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = a.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                approval_counts[date_only] = approval_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        daily_data = []\n        for entry in sorted(analytics_entries, key=lambda e: e.date):\n            date = entry.date\n            daily_data.append({\n                'date': date,\n                'views': entry.views,\n                'comments': comment_counts.get(date, 0),\n                'approvals': approval_counts.get(date, 0)\n            })\n\n        return {\n            'total_views': total_views,\n            'total_unique_visitors': total_unique,\n            'average_time_seconds': round(average_time, 2),\n            'total_shares': total_shares,\n            'daily_data': daily_data\n        }\n\n\n# Simple input validation (only very basic validations)\n\ndef validate_article_form(form):\n    errors = {}\n    title = form.get('title','').strip()\n    category = form.get('category','').strip()\n    if not title:\n        errors['title'] = \"Title cannot be empty.\"\n    if not category:\n        errors['category'] = \"Category cannot be empty.\"\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_schedule_form(form):\n    errors = {}\n    scheduled_date = form.get('scheduled_date','').strip()\n    article_id = form.get('article_id','').strip()\n    if not scheduled_date:\n        errors['scheduled_date'] = \"Scheduled date is required.\"\n    if not article_id:\n        errors['article_id'] = \"Article ID is required.\"\n    else:\n        try:\n            int(article_id)\n        except:\n            errors['article_id'] = \"Article ID must be a number.\"\n    return errors\n\n\n# Flask app factory\napp = Flask(__name__)\napp.secret_key = 'supersecretkey'\n\n\n# Before request - set user in g from session\n@app.before_request\ndef       self.article_id = int(article_id)\n        self.scheduled_date = scheduled_date\n        self.notes = notes\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        records = 'scheduled_date': self.scheduled_date,\n            'notes': self.notes\n        }\n\n    def save(self):\n        entries = CalendarEntry.load_all()\n        updated = False\n        for i,e in enumerate(entries):\n            if e.calendar_id == self.calendar_id:\n                entries[i] = self\n                updated = True\n                break\n        if not updated:\n            entries.append(self)\n        records = [e.to_dict() for e in entries]\n        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS, records)\n\n\n@app.route('/calendar', methods=['GET','POST'])\ndef content_calendar():\n    if not g.user:\n        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        form = request.form\n        errors = 'workflow_stages.txt')  # Utility functions for file I/O and data parsing  def read_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames):     records = []     if not os.path.exists(filename):         return records     with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for line in f:             line=line.strip('\n\r ')             if not line:                 continue             parts = line.split('|')             if len(parts) != len(fieldnames):                 # skip malformed line                 continue             record = dict(zip(fieldnames, parts))             records.append(record)     return records   def append_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, record_dict):     values = []     for field in fieldnames:         val = record_dict.get(field, '')         if val is None:             val = ''         if isinstance(val, str):             val = val.replace('\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')         values.append(str(val))     with open(filename, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:         f.write('|'.join(values)+'\n')   def overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, records):     with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:         for rec AnalyticsService:     @staticmethod     def get_analytics_for_article(article_id):         analytics_entries = AnalyticsEntry.find_by_article(article_id)         # Aggregate overall data         total_views = sum(entry.views for entry in analytics_entries)         total_unique = sum(entry.unique_visitors for entry in analytics_entries)         average_time = 0         if analytics_entries:             average_time = sum(entry.avg_time_seconds * entry.views for entry in analytics_entries) / max(total_views,1)         total_shares = sum(entry.shares for entry in analytics_entries)          # Calculate comments and approvals counts per date         comments = Comment.load_all()         approvals = Approval.load_all()          comment_counts = {}         approval_counts = {}          for c in comments:             if c.article_id == int(article_id):                 date_only = c.timestamp.split('T')[0]                 comment_counts[date_only] = comment_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1          for a in approvals:             if a.article_id == int(article_id):                 date_only = a.timestamp.split('T')[0]                 approval_counts[date_only] = approval_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1          daily_data = []         for entry in sorted(analytics_entries, key=lambda e: e.date):             date = entry.date             daily_data.append({                 'date': date,                 'views': entry.views,                 'comments': comment_counts.get(date, 0),                 'approvals': approval_counts.get(date, 0)             })          return {             'total_views': total_views,             'total_unique_visitors': total_unique,             'average_time_seconds': round(average_time, 2),             'total_shares': selected_sort=sort)   # Calendar scheduling CALENDAR_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'calendar.txt') CALENDAR_FIELDS = ['calendar_id','article_id','scheduled_date','notes']   class CalendarEntry:     def __init__(self, calendar_id, article_id, scheduled_date, notes):         self.calendar_id = int(calendar_id)         self.article_id = int(article_id)         self.scheduled_date = scheduled_date         self.notes = notes      @classmethod     def load_all(cls):         records = read_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS)         result = []         for rec in records:             try:                 result.append(CalendarEntry(**rec))             except Exception:                 continue         return result      @classmethod     def next_calendar_id(cls):         entries = cls.load_all()         if not entries:             return 1         return max(e.calendar_id for e in entries) + 1      def to_dict(self):         return {             'calendar_id': self.calendar_id,             'article_id': self.article_id,             'scheduled_date': self.scheduled_date,             'notes': self.notes         }      def save(self):         entries = CalendarEntry.load_all()         updated = False         for i,e in enumerate(entries):             if e.calendar_id == self.calendar_id:                 entries[i] = self                 updated = True                 break         if not updated:             entries.append(self)         records = [e.to_dict() for e in entries]         overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS, records) CalendarEntry(calendar_id, article_id, scheduled_date, notes)         calendar_entry.save()         return redirect(url_for('content_calendar'))     else:         scheduled_items =   [feedback_testing.txt] Functional Testing Report for Content Publishing Hub  1. Dashboard Page - Access without login redirects to root as expected. - Access with login shows dashboard with div id="dashboard-page" and personalized welcome.  2. Article Creation (/article/create) - GET loads create article page correctly. - POST with empty title and category returns validation errors as expected with messages "Title cannot be empty.", "Category cannot be empty.". - POST with valid data redirects to edit page with new article id included.  3. Article Editing (/article/<article_id>/edit) - GET loads the edit article page with div id="edit-article-page". - POST to update title, category, and new content creates a new version and updates main article. - Redirect after POST is successful. - Article title and latest version number updated correctly.  4. Version History (/article/<article_id>/versions) - Loads version history page with div id="version-history-page" and table with id="version-history-table". - Displays correct number of versions including newly added ones.  5. My Articles (/articles/mine) - Shows table with id="my-articles-table" listing user's articles. - Article entries with titles present.  6. Published Articles (/articles/published) - Displays published articles filtered by category and sorted correctly. - Table with id="published-articles-table" shown and data displayed.  7. Content Calendar (/calendar) - GET loads content calendar page with div id="content-calendar-page". - POST with missing scheduled_date shows validation error. - POST with invalid article_id shows validation error. - POST with valid data redirects and saves scheduled item.  8. Article Analytics (/article/<article_id>/analytics) - Loads analytics page with div id="article-analytics-page".  Defects and Issues: - Several backend operations that write files (e.g., creating articles or versions) failed due to missing data directory or files (FileNotFoundError for 'data/articles.txt' and similar). - This implies that the data directory 'data' and necessary data files must exist before the app is used. - The dashboard template refers to 'analytics_article_id' variable for analytics link but no such variable provided in route leading to possible rendering errors. - Templates like content_calendar.html refer to variables like 'unscheduled_articles' and 'scheduled_items' with attributes like 'article_title' and 'schedule_date', but those are not fully supported or mapped in the current backend code. - The article_analytics.html template uses variables 'analytics_data' in a loop but backend sends 'analytics' dictionary, mismatch in variable names may cause rendering issues. - The analytics table in article_analytics.html shows columns for Comments and Approvals but there is no code populating 'comments' or 'approvals' counts in the analytics data passed, these columns may appear empty.  Suggestions: - Ensure 'data' directory and all necessary files exist or are created on app startup or deployment. - Fix the dashboard route to provide 'analytics_article_id' or adjust template to handle missing variable gracefully. - Check content calendar data preparation to supply correct article lists for scheduling. - Fix variable naming mismatch in article analytics template (use 'analytics.daily_data' for the loop if that holds data). - Backend should enhance analytics data to include comments and approval counts or remove those columns from the analytics table.  Status: NEED_MODIFY  This report summarizes the main functional coverage and discovered defects to guide the ImplementationImprover for corrections.

[Tool_Request] ImplementationImprover-Failover-Reliable: write_text_file({"filename": "app.py", "content": "from flask import Flask, request, session, g, redirect, url_for, render_template, abort\nimport os\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\n# Data directory and filenames\nDATA_DIR = 'data'\nUSERS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'users.txt')\nARTICLES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'articles.txt')\nARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'article_versions.txt')\nAPPROVALS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'approvals.txt')\nCOMMENTS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'comments.txt')\nANALYTICS_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'analytics.txt')\nWORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'workflow_stages.txt')\nCALENDAR_FILE = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'calendar.txt')\n\n# Utility functions for file I/O and data parsing\n\ndef read_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames):\n    records = []\n    if not os.path.exists(filename):\n        return records\n    with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line=line.strip('\\n\\r ')\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != len(fieldnames):\n                # skip malformed line\n                continue\n            record = dict(zip(fieldnames, parts))\n            records.append(record)\n    return records\n\n\ndef append_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, record_dict):\n    values = []\n    for field in fieldnames:\n        val = record_dict.get(field, '')\n        if val is None:\n            val = ''\n        if isinstance(val, str):\n            val = val.replace('\\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')\n        values.append(str(val))\n    with open(filename, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('|'.join(values)+'\\n')\n\n\ndef overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, records):\n    with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for rec in records:\n            values = []\n            for field in fieldnames:\n                val = rec.get(field, '')\n                if val is None:\n                    val = ''\n                if isinstance(val, str):\n                    val = val.replace('\\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')\n                values.append(str(val))\n            f.write('|'.join(values)+'\\n')\n\n\n# Models\n\n# User model\nclass User:\n    FIELDS = ['username', 'fullname', 'email']\n\n    def __init__(self, username, fullname, email):\n        self.username = username\n        self.fullname = fullname\n        self.email = email\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        users = read_pipe_delimited_file(USERS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        return [cls(**u) for u in users]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_username(cls, username):\n        all_users = cls.load_all()\n        for user in all_users:\n            if user.username == username:\n                return user\n        return None\n\n\n# Article model\nclass Article:\n    # Fields as per articles.txt\n    # article_id|title|category|author|status|created_at|updated_at\n    FIELDS = ['article_id','title','category','author','status','created_at','updated_at']\n\n    def __init__(self, article_id, title, category, author, status, created_at, updated_at):\n        self.article_id = int(article_id)\n        self.title = title\n        self.category = category\n        self.author = author\n        self.status = status\n        self.created_at = created_at\n        self.updated_at = updated_at\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        articles = read_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLES_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for a in articles:\n            try:\n                result.append(Article(**a))\n            except Exception:\n                # malformed line\n                continue\n        return result\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_id(cls, article_id):\n        if not isinstance(article_id, int):\n            try:\n                article_id = int(article_id)\n            except:\n                return None\n        for a in cls.load_all():\n            if a.article_id == article_id:\n                return a\n        return None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_author(cls, author):\n        return [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.author == author]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_published(cls, category_filter=None, sort_key=None):\n        articles = [a for a in cls.load_all() if a.status == 'published']\n        if category_filter:\n            articles = [a for a in articles if a.category == category_filter]\n        if sort_key == 'created_at' or sort_key == 'date':\n            articles.sort(key=lambda x:x.created_at, reverse=True)\n        elif sort_key == 'title':\n            articles.sort(key=lambda x:x.title)\n        return articles\n\n    @classmethod\n    def next_article_id(cls):\n        articles = cls.load_all()\n        if not articles:\n            return 1\n        max_id = max(a.article_id for a in articles)\n        return max_id + 1\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            'article_id': self.article_id,\n            'title': self.title,\n            'category': self.category,\n            'author': self.author,\n            'status': self.status,\n            'created_at': self.created_at,\n            'updated_at': self.updated_at\n        }\n\n    def save(self):\n        all_articles = Article.load_all()\n        updated = False\n        for i,a in enumerate(all_articles):\n            if a.article_id == self.article_id:\n                all_articles[i] = self\n                updated = True\n                break\n        if not updated:\n            all_articles.append(self)\n        # Write back\n        records = [a.to_dict() for a in all_articles]\n        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLES_FILE, Article.FIELDS, records)\n\n\n# ArticleVersion model\nclass ArticleVersion:\n    # article_versions.txt fields:\n    # version_id|article_id|version_number|content|author|timestamp|change_summary\n    FIELDS = ['version_id','article_id','version_number','content','author','timestamp','change_summary']\n\n    def __init__(self, version_id, article_id, version_number, content, author, timestamp, change_summary):\n        self.version_id = int(version_id)\n        self.article_id = int(article_id)\n        self.version_number = int(version_number)\n        self.content = content\n        self.author = author\n        self.timestamp = timestamp\n        self.change_summary = change_summary\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for rec in records:\n            try:\n                result.append(ArticleVersion(**rec))\n            except Exception:\n                continue\n        return result\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_article(cls, article_id):\n        return [v for v in cls.load_all() if v.article_id == int(article_id)]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_latest_version_number(cls, article_id):\n        versions = cls.find_by_article(article_id)\n        if not versions:\n            return 0\n        return max(v.version_number for v in versions)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def find_by_version_id(cls, version_id):\n        for v in cls.load_all():\n            if v.version_id == int(version_id):\n                return v\n        return None\n\n    @classmethod\n    def next_version_id(cls):\n        versions = cls.load_all()\n        if not versions:\n            return 1\n        return max(v.version_id for v in versions) + 1\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            'version_id': self.version_id,\n            'article_id': self.article_id,\n            'version_number': self.version_number,\n            'content': self.content,\n            'author': self.author,\n            'timestamp': self.timestamp,\n            'change_summary': self.change_summary\n        }\n\n    def save(self):\n        versions = ArticleVersion.load_all()\n        updated = False\n        for i,v in enumerate(versions):\n            if v.version_id == self.version_id:\n                versions[i] = self\n                updated = True\n                break\n        if not updated:\n            versions.append(self)\n        records = [v.to_dict() for v in versions]\n        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(ARTICLE_VERSIONS_FILE, ArticleVersion.FIELDS, records)\n\n\n# Approval model\nclass Approval:\n    # approvals.txt fields:\n    # return max(c.comment_id for c in comments) + 1\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            'comment_id': self.comment_id,\n            'article_id': self.article_id,\n     comments = Comment.load_all()\n        updated = False\n        for i,c in enumerate(comments):\n            if c.comment_id == self.comment_id:\n                comments[i] = self\n overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(COMMENTS_FILE, Comment.FIELDS, records)\n\n\n# Analytics model\nclass AnalyticsEntry:\n    # analytics.txt fields:\n    # stage_id|category|stage_order|description\n    FIELDS = ['stage_id','category','stage_order','description']\n\n    def __init__(self, stage_id, category, stage_order, description):\n  records = read_pipe_delimited_file(WORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE, cls.FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for rec in records:\n            try:\n                result.append(WorkflowStage(**rec))\n            except:\n                continue\n        return result\n\n\n# Business logic services\n\n\nclass ArticleService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def create_article(title, category, author):\n        new_id = Article.next_article_id()\n        now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article = Article(\n            article_id=new_id,\n            title=title,\n            category=category,\n            author=author,\n            status='draft',\n            created_at=now,\n            updated_at=now\n        )\n        article.save()\n        # Create initial version empty with version_number 1\n        version = ArticleVersion(\n            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),\n            article_id=new_id,\n            version_number=1,\n            content='',\n            author=author,\n            timestamp=now,\n            change_summary=\"Initial version\"\n        )\n        version.save()\n        return article\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def update_article(article_id, title, category):\n        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)\n        if not article:\n            return None\n        article.title = title\n        article.category = category\n        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article.save()\n        return article\n\n\nclass VersionService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def create_version(article_id, content, author, change_summary):\n        article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)\n        if not article:\n            return None\n        next_version_num = ArticleVersion.find_latest_version_number(article_id) + 1\n        version = ArticleVersion(\n            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_number=next_version_num,\n            content=content,\n            author=author,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            change_summary=change_summary\n        )\n        version.save()\n        # Update article updated_at\n        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article.save()\n        return version\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_versions(article_id):\n        return sorted(ArticleVersion.find_by_article(article_id), key=lambda v: v.version_number)\n\n\nclass ApprovalService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_approval(article_id, version_id, approver, status, comments):\n        approval_id = Approval.next_approval_id()\n        approval = Approval(\n            approval_id=approval_id,\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_id=version_id,\n            approver=approver,\n            status=status,\n            comments=comments,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        approval.save()\n        return approval\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_approvals(article_id, version_id):\n        return Approval.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)\n\n\nclass CommentService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_comment(article_id, version_id, commenter, comment_text):\n        comment_id = Comment.next_comment_id()\n        comment = Comment(\n            comment_id=comment_id,\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_id=version_id,\n            commenter=commenter,\n            comment_text=comment_text,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        comment.save()\n        return comment\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_comments(article_id, version_id):\n        return Comment.find_by_article_and_version(article_id, version_id)\n\n\nclass AnalyticsService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_analytics_for_article(article_id):\n        analytics_entries = AnalyticsEntry.find_by_article(article_id)\n        # Aggregate overall data\n        total_views = sum(entry.views for entry in analytics_entries)\n        total_unique = sum(entry.unique_visitors for entry in analytics_entries)\n        average_time = 0\n        if analytics_entries:\n            average_time = sum(entry.avg_time_seconds * entry.views for entry in analytics_entries) / max(total_views,1)\n        total_shares = sum(entry.shares for entry in analytics_entries)\n\n        # Calculate comments and approvals counts per date\n        comments = Comment.load_all()\n        approvals = Approval.load_all()\n\n        comment_counts = {}\n        approval_counts = {}\n\n        for c in comments:\n            if c.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = c.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                comment_counts[date_only] = comment_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        for a in approvals:\n            if a.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = a.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                approval_counts[date_only] = approval_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        daily_data = []\n        for entry in sorted(analytics_entries, key=lambda e: e.date):\n            date = entry.date\n            daily_data.append({\n                'date': date,\n                'views': entry.views,\n                'comments': comment_counts.get(date, 0),\n                'approvals': approval_counts.get(date, 0)\n            })\n\n        return {\n            'total_views': total_views,\n            'total_unique_visitors': total_unique,\n            'average_time_seconds': round(average_time, 2),\n            'total_shares': total_shares,\n            'daily_data': daily_data\n        }\n\n\n# Simple input validation (only very basic validations)\n\ndef validate_article_form(form):\n    errors = {}\n    title = form.get('title','').strip()\n    category = form.get('category','').strip()\n    if not title:\n        errors['title'] = \"Title cannot be empty.\"\n    if not category:\n        errors['category'] = \"Category cannot be empty.\"\n    return errors\n\n\ndef validate_schedule_form(form):\n    errors = {}\n    scheduled_date = form.get('scheduled_date','').strip()\n    article_id = form.get('article_id','').strip()\n    if not scheduled_date:\n        errors['scheduled_date'] = \"Scheduled date is required.\"\n    if not article_id:\n        errors['article_id'] = \"Article ID is required.\"\n    else:\n        try:\n            int(article_id)\n        except:\n            errors['article_id'] = \"Article ID must be a number.\"\n    return errors\n\n\n# Flask app factory\napp = Flask(__name__)\napp.secret_key = 'supersecretkey'\n\n\n# Before request - set user in g from session\n@app.before_request\ndef load_logged_in_user():\n    g.user = None\n    username = session.get('username')\n    if username:\n        user = User.find_by_username(username)\n        if user:\n            g.user = user\n\n\n# COMMENTS_FILE, ANALYTICS_FILE, WORKFLOW_STAGES_FILE, CALENDAR_FILE]\nfor fpath in required_files:\n    if not os.path.exists(fpath):\n        open(fpath, 'a').close()\n\n\n# Root redirect\n@app.route('/')\ndef root_redirect():\n    return redirect(url_for('dashboard'))\n\n\n# Route: /dashboard\n@app.route('/dashboard')\ndef dashboard():\n    if not g.user:\n        return analytics_article_id=analytics_article_id)\n\n\n# Route: /article/create (GET, POST)\n@app.route('/article/create', methods=['GET','POST'])\ndef create_article():\n    if not g.user:\n        return 'article' to match template\n            return render_template('create_article.html', validation_errors=errors, article=form)\n        title = form.get('title','').strip()\n        category = methods=['GET','POST'])\ndef edit_article(article_id):\n    if not g.user:\n        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))\n    article = Article.find_by_id(article_id)\n    if not article:\n        abort(404)\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        form = request.form\n        errors = validate_article_form(form)\n        if errors:\n            # Provide form and article to template\n            return render_template('edit_article.html', validation_errors=errors, article=article, form=form)\n\n        # update article main info\n        updated_article = ArticleService.update_article(article_id, form.get('title','').strip(), form.get('category','').strip())\n\n        # Create new version if content or change_summary provided\n        content = form.get('content','').strip()\n        change_summary = form.get('change_summary','').strip()\n\n        if content or change_summary:\n            if not change_summary:\n                change_summary = \"Updated content\"\n            VersionService.create_version(article_id, content, g.user.username, change_summary)\n\n        return redirect(url_for('edit_article', article_id=article_id))\n\n    else:\n        versions = VersionService.get_versions(article_id)\n        latest_version = versions[-1] if versions else None\n        content_value = latest_version.content if latest_version else ''\n        return render_template('edit_article.html', article=article, current_version=latest_version, content=content_value)\n\n\n# Route:           'author': art.author,\n            'category': art.category,\n            'publish_date': art.updated_at\n        })\n    return render_template('published_articles.html', articles=articles, categories=categories, selected_category=category, selected_sort=sort)\n\n\n# Calendar scheduling\nCALENDAR_FIELDS = ['calendar_id','article_id','scheduled_date','notes']\n\n\nclass CalendarEntry:\n    def __init__(self, calendar_id, article_id, scheduled_date, notes):\n        self.calendar_id = int(calendar_id)\n        self.article_id = int(article_id)\n        self.scheduled_date = scheduled_date\n        self.notes = notes\n\n    @classmethod\n    def load_all(cls):\n        records = read_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS)\n        result = []\n        for rec in records:\n            try:\n                result.append(CalendarEntry(**rec))\n            except Exception:\n                continue\n        return result\n\n    @classmethod\n    def next_calendar_id(cls):\n        entries = cls.load_all()\n        if not entries:\n            return 1\n        return max(e.calendar_id for e in entries) + 1\n\n    def to_dict(self):\n        return {\n            'calendar_id': self.calendar_id,\n            'article_id': self.article_id,\n            'scheduled_date': self.scheduled_date,\n            'notes': self.notes\n        }\n\n    def save(self):\n        entries = CalendarEntry.load_all()\n        updated = False\n        for i,e in enumerate(entries):\n            if e.calendar_id == self.calendar_id:\n                entries[i] = self\n                updated = True\n                break\n        if not updated:\n            entries.append(self)\n        records = [e.to_dict() for e in entries]\n        overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(CALENDAR_FILE, CALENDAR_FIELDS, records)\n\n\n@app.route('/calendar', methods=['GET','POST'])\ndef content_calendar():\n    if not g.user:\n        return redirect(url_for('root_redirect'))\n    if request.method == 'POST':\n        form = request.form\n        errors = validate_schedule_form(form)\n        if errors:\n            scheduled_items = CalendarEntry.load_all()\n            articles_map = {a.article_id:a.title for a in Article.load_all()}\n            scheduled_items_display = []\n            for item in scheduled_items:\n                scheduled_items_display.append({\n                    'id': item.calendar_id,\n                    'article_title': articles_map.get(item.article_id, 'Unknown'),\n                    'schedule_date': item.scheduled_date\n                })\n            return render_template('content_calendar.html', validation_errors=errors, scheduled_items=scheduled_items_display, unscheduled_articles=[])\n\n        article_id = int(form.get('article_id').strip())\n        scheduled_date = form.get('scheduled_date').strip()\n        notes = form.get('notes','').strip()\n records\n    with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line=line.strip('\\n\\r ')\n            if not line:\n                continue\n            parts = line.split('|')\n            if len(parts) != len(fieldnames):\n                # skip malformed line\n                continue\n            record = dict(zip(fieldnames, parts))\n            records.append(record)\n    return records\n\n\ndef append_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, record_dict):\n    values = []\n    for field in fieldnames:\n        val = record_dict.get(field, '')\n        if val is None:\n            val = ''\n        if isinstance(val, str):\n            val = val.replace('\\n', ' ').replace('|', ' ')\n        values.append(str(val))\n    with open(filename, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        f.write('|'.join(values)+'\\n')\n\n\ndef overwrite_pipe_delimited_file(filename, fieldnames, records):\n    with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for rec in records:\n            values = []\n            for field in fieldnames:\n                val = rec.get(field, '')\n                if val is None:\n ArticleVersion.find_latest_version_number(article_id) + 1\n        version = ArticleVersion(\n            version_id=ArticleVersion.next_version_id(),\n            article_id=article_id,\n        version.save()\n        # Update article updated_at\n        article.updated_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        article.save()\n        return version\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_versions(article_id):\n        return sorted(ArticleVersion.find_by_article(article_id), key=lambda v: v.version_number)\n\n\nclass ApprovalService:\n    @staticmethod\n    def add_approval(article_id, version_id, approver, status, comments):\n        approval_id = Approval.next_approval_id()\n        approval = Approval(\n            approval_id=approval_id,\n            article_id=article_id,\n            version_id=version_id,\n            approver=approver,\n            status=status,\n            comments=comments,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        approval.save()\n        return approval\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_approvals(article_id, version_id):\n        return         comment_text=comment_text,\n            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        )\n        comment.save()\n        return comment\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_analytics_for_article(article_id):\n        analytics_entries = AnalyticsEntry.find_by_article(article_id)\n        # Aggregate overall data\n        total_views = sum(entry.views for entry in analytics_entries)\n        total_unique = sum(entry.unique_visitors for entry in analytics_entries)\n        average_time = 0\n        if analytics_entries:\n            average_time = sum(entry.avg_time_seconds * entry.views for entry in analytics_entries) / max(total_views,1)\n        total_shares = sum(entry.shares for entry in analytics_entries)\n\n        # Calculate comments and approvals counts per date\n        comments = Comment.load_all()\n        approvals = Approval.load_all()\n\n        comment_counts = {}\n        approval_counts = {}\n\n        for c in comments:\n            if c.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = c.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                comment_counts[date_only] = comment_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        for a in approvals:\n            if a.article_id == int(article_id):\n                date_only = a.timestamp.split('T')[0]\n                approval_counts[date_only] = approval_counts.get(date_only, 0) + 1\n\n        daily_data = []\n        for entry in sorted(analytics_entries, key=lambda e: e.date):\n            date = entry.date\n            daily_data.append({\n                'date': date,\n                'views': entry.views,\n                'comments': comment_counts.get(date, 0),\n                'approvals': approval_counts.get(date, 0)\n            })\n\n        return {\n            'total_views': total_views,\n            'total_unique_visitors': total_unique,\n            'average_time_seconds': round(average_time, 2),\n            'total_shares': total_shares,\n            'daily_data': daily_data\n        }\n\n\n# Simple input validation (only very basic validations)\n\ndef entries[i] = self\n                updated = True\n                break\n        if not updated:\n            entries.append(self)\n        records = [e.to_dict() for e in entries]\n
